title: Explaining the Soul to an Ex-Atheist - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request from an ex-atheist asking about the soul, consciousness, and the double-slit experiment. He shares his views on why people become atheists, critiques both fundamentalist Christianity and militant atheism, argues for reincarnation (including 'time travel reincarnation'), and discusses how science and spirituality can be reconciled through concepts like past-life memories, energy transfer, and the double-slit experiment as evidence of a spiritual dimension.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: atheists are often reactionary and close-minded, rejecting God as a reaction to bad religion rather than from genuine reasoning; militant atheism is itself a religion
the soul is real, eternal, and distinct from the body; we are souls inhabiting bodies, not the body itself
reincarnation is true and he personally believes in 'time travel reincarnation' where souls can incarnate into any time period
both mainstream camps are wrong; natural selection is valid but doesn't preclude God; intelligent design proponents are wrong to rely solely on the Old Testament
scientists are often narrow technicians who miss the big picture; too much societal reverence for science has blinded people to spiritual reality
it demonstrates the universe is more complex than science can explain and supports a spiritual dimension; it cannot be explained away
genetic modification expands rather than diminishes individuality; souls choose their bodies and programming, so more options mean more conscious soul selection
oil comes from inside a growing earth as a byproduct of growth, not from dinosaur remains; mainstream geology has this wrong
fundamentalist Christianity is dangerously literal and wrong, but the core spiritual message of Jesus about the soul and God is valid
over-emphasis on scientific/technical education at the expense of spiritual and holistic thinking is a global problem

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I used to be an atheist until I had the strangest thought of my life.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believed science was able to explain the nature of reality.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I became interested in the concepts of Darwin and the theories of natural selection to explain the development of humans in the natural world.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When looking at a dog, I was able to explain why it looked and behaved in a certain way based upon centuries of selective breeding by humans.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I understood how living creatures had DNA which determined physical attributes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had an epiphany when I realized I could explain the existence of everything external but not my own consciousness.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I started trying to remember back into my childhood to figure out when I first realized I was conscious of my existence.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Even on the scale of a single sperm and egg, leading towards our eventual consciousness is bizarre and improbable.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Even though we don't have any scientific evidence for a soul, I wonder if the soul provides the answer to our own unique individual existence.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The computer you use was produced in a factory and serves the purpose we assign it, but in human terms, you exist without understanding what your thoughts program or originated.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was wondering if the fact we exist at all is so unlikely and inexplicable that it proves there are much greater forces at play than we can possibly explain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think modern atheists are generally smarter than fundamentalist Christians.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Atheists are people who don't have a good education in the religious area.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Atheists see religion as only the negative side because they get all their information from school, which doesn't include any religion.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Atheists reject God and the soul partly as a reaction to the bad things religion has done.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Atheists get mesmerized by the scientific side because of the strides made in science over the last couple hundred years.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Science is all about learning the rules and doing experiments; you don't have to be that smart to be a scientist.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We look up to scientists so much as a society because of all the stuff science does and because scientifically advanced countries have a huge economic advantage.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Scientists are just technicians; they're not people you want to be looking up to.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Our focus on scientific advancements can block out all the other stuff, like religion.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Science doesn't explain the spiritual side at all, except maybe the double-slit experiment.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: People can get addicted to certain mindsets, like being addicted to a video game.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've had conversations about the double-slit experiment, and some scientists can't explain it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I met a geologist who had never heard of the growing earth theory, which is Geology 101.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Oil comes from inside the earth, not from dead dinosaurs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The earth is a living being that grows from the inside, just like a plant.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Scientists are looked up to, but they're just technicians.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Our educational system worldwide puts too much faith in technology and not enough on the big picture.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We need to take the next step in science by integrating data from all different fields.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The mainstream intelligent design and evolution arguments are both wrong.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Intelligent design people are idiots because they get their ideas from the Old Testament.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Atheists who believe in natural selection don't have to be atheists; it's not just a random accident.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: People need to remember that blind faith in religion is a rejection of evidence and logic.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There is scientific support for the afterlife, like past life memories.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Children can remember their past lives, like a little boy who remembered his past life in World War II.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We all have stories of communicating with people in other situations because we've been in that situation in past lives.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Atheists can be closed-minded because they're raised religiously but don't talk about religion, so they reject it entirely.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Religious people need to acknowledge the negative side of religion to have better conversations with atheists.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: DNA relates to your mind and the physical world, but we don't know how much is affected by your mind.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Genetically engineering a person takes away their individuality, but it's always been like that with parents programming their children.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Children choose their parents just as much as parents program their children.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Genetic modification expands our power; it doesn't take it away.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We've always known that we're not our bodies; we live inside our bodies.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Reincarnation is a human being reincarnating as a human, not as a star or something else.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Time travel reincarnation is a belief I've seriously contemplated over the last four years.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: People reincarnate where they belong, with the people they relate to.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Atheism is a religion because atheists claim to know the soul is extinguished when you die, which they can't prove.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The law of transference of energy suggests the soul doesn't disappear when the body dies.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There are scientific experiments connecting the spirit world with the physical world, like DNA unraveling when you get stressed out.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Psychic stuff is real, like people having their own personal source or detecting changes in the electromagnetic field when major events happen.
